_metadata.description: Configurable scoring rulebook for Indian Mutual Fund advisory engine. All weights, thresholds, and rules are defined here. No scoring logic lives in LLM prompts.
changelog: v2.0.0 (2026-03-01): Full multi-factor scorecard with qualitative module

scoring_architecture.description: Two-layer scoring: Python computes deterministic quantitative scores. LLM computes qualitative scores using structured rubrics. Final score is a weighted blend.
scoring_architecture.note: Qualitative weight increases to 0.40 if factsheet data is available (qualitative_boost_if_factsheet_available = true)

quantitative_scorecard._description: Each dimension is scored 0-10. Dimension scores are weighted to produce a composite quantitative score (0-10).

alpha_generation.description: Measures the fund manager's true value-add over the benchmark. Rewards consistent alpha across time horizons.

alpha_3y.description: 3-year alpha is the primary signal — captures recent manager skill.

alpha_5y.description: 5-year alpha validates structural skill vs. lucky cycles.
alpha_5y.na_handling: If 5Y data is unavailable, use alpha_3y score with a 0.10 penalty applied to the dimension score.

risk_adjusted_returns.description: Rewards funds that generate returns efficiently without excessive volatility.

sharpe_ratio.description: Primary risk-adjusted metric. Higher is better.

sortino_ratio.description: Penalizes only downside volatility. Better signal than Sharpe for equity funds.
sortino_ratio.na_handling: If unavailable, use Sharpe score as proxy.

beta.description: Market sensitivity. Penalty for taking excessive market risk without commensurate returns.

category_override.description: Beta tolerance varies by category. A Small Cap fund with Beta=1.2 is normal. A Large Cap fund with Beta=1.2 is a red flag.

cost_efficiency.description: Expense ratio directly reduces investor returns. Penalizes expensive funds, rewards lean ones.

expense_ratio_vs_category_norm.scoring_logic: Compare expense_ratio to the category norm thresholds above. Score 10 if below 'low', scale linearly to 0 if above 'high'.

return_consistency.description: Rolling return consistency separates funds that outperform randomly vs. structurally. This is the most under-valued metric by retail investors.

rolling_cagr_vs_peer_median.description: Rolling 3Y CAGR vs. peer median. Above-peer consistency is a structural alpha signal.

peer_percentile_rank.description: Peer rank percentile (0=worst, 100=best). From Kaggle dataset.
peer_percentile_rank.na_handling: If peer rank unavailable, use rolling_cagr_vs_peer_median score for full weight of this dimension.

qualitative_scorecard._description: LLM evaluates each dimension using structured rubrics from factsheet commentary. Score is 0-10 per dimension.
qualitative_scorecard._important: LLM MUST return scores as structured JSON — not narrative text. Narrative is generated separately after scoring.

manager_stability_and_track_record.description: Fund manager continuity is a strong predictor of consistent returns. Manager changes reset the performance clock.

llm_rubric.score_10: Same manager for 5+ years with stated conviction in strategy. Explicit stock rationale provided.
llm_rubric.score_7: Manager has 3-5 years tenure. Commentary is coherent and strategy-aligned.
llm_rubric.score_5: Manager changed within 2 years OR commentary is generic with no specific rationale.
llm_rubric.score_2: Recent manager change AND no clear strategy articulation. Performance continuity at risk.
llm_rubric.score_0: No manager information available OR multiple manager changes in 2 years.

style_integrity.description: The most critical qualitative check. Style drift (e.g., a 'Value' fund buying high-PE growth stocks) is a silent risk that only manifests in drawdowns.

llm_rubric.score_10: Commentary perfectly matches stated style. A 'value' fund cites low PE/PB. A 'growth' fund cites earnings acceleration. Zero drift signals.
llm_rubric.score_7: Commentary broadly aligns with style. Minor deviations are acknowledged and explained.
llm_rubric.score_5: Commentary is style-neutral or vague. Cannot confirm alignment.
llm_rubric.score_2: Commentary contains language inconsistent with stated style (e.g., a 'value' fund discussing momentum stocks). Possible drift.
llm_rubric.score_0: Clear style contradiction. Fund is doing the opposite of what it claims (e.g., 'Mid Cap' fund holding 60%+ large caps).

amc_governance_and_outlook.description: AMC-level signals: regulatory actions, AUM growth/decline, fee transparency, and macro outlook accuracy.

llm_rubric.score_10: AMC has been SEBI compliant with no recent actions. AUM is growing. Macro outlook is specific, data-backed, and nuanced.
llm_rubric.score_7: No regulatory issues. Outlook is reasonable but generic.
llm_rubric.score_5: Outlook is absent or copy-pasted boilerplate with no original analysis.
llm_rubric.score_2: Macro outlook contradicts available economic data. Or AMC has had a minor SEBI observation in the last 2 years.
llm_rubric.score_0: AMC has a SEBI enforcement action in the last 12 months. Or fund is under regulatory scrutiny.

known_amc_flags._note: Populate this list with AMCs having recent regulatory actions. Checked before LLM call.

investor_fit_module.description: Adjusts the final score based on fit with the investor's specific profile. Applied AFTER base scores are computed. This is a multiplier/modifier, not a separate dimension.

risk_mismatch_penalty.description: Penalize funds where fund risk profile grossly mismatches investor risk appetite.

rules.reason: High volatility fund for conservative investor

rules.condition: fund_category == 'Debt Short' AND investor_risk == 'Aggressive' AND investor_horizon_years > 10
rules.reason: Suboptimal allocation for long-horizon aggressive investor

rules.condition: fund_category in ['Index Fund', 'Large Cap'] AND investor_risk == 'Aggressive' AND investor_age < 40

horizon_mismatch_penalty.description: Short-term investors in long-duration or volatile funds are penalized.

rules.condition: fund_category in ['Mid Cap', 'Flexi Cap'] AND investor_horizon_years < 3

diversification_bonus.description: Reward a fund if it genuinely diversifies the portfolio (different category, low overlap).
diversification_bonus.condition: fund adds a unique category absent in other 2 funds

concentration_penalty.description: If 2 or more of the top 3 funds are in the same category, penalize the duplicate.
concentration_penalty.condition: fund category duplicates another fund in the top-3

verdict_thresholds.description: Final score (0-10) maps to verdicts. These thresholds are configurable.
verdict_thresholds._note: Verdicts are determined by final_score = (quant_score * quant_weight) + (qual_score * qual_weight) + investor_fit_modifiers

BUY_MORE.description: Strong conviction fund — increase allocation on next SIP review.

HOLD.description: Performing adequately. No action needed but monitor quarterly.

REDUCE.description: Structurally weak. Reduce SIP by 50% and reassess in 2 quarters.

EXIT.description: Exit and redeploy into a higher-scoring fund in the same category.

override_rules.description: Hard rules that can force a verdict regardless of score. Applied last.

rules.name: Catastrophic Alpha Override
rules.reason: Chronic severe underperformance — no quantitative recovery path.

rules.name: Style Drift Critical Override
rules.reason: Style drift detected — future returns are unpredictable.

rules.name: New Fund Caution
rules.reason: Insufficient track record. Cannot issue BUY MORE on funds under 3 years old.

rules.name: Manager Honeymoon Override
rules.reason: New manager. Cannot assess skill with <1 year of data.

macro_rebalancing_rules.description: Age-based target allocation bands. Used by the Macro Asset Allocation Strategist agent.

rebalancing_trigger.description: Flag for rebalancing if any asset class deviates from ideal by this many percentage points.

data_quality_rules.description: Governs how missing or stale data affects scoring confidence.
data_quality_rules.na_handling_policy: Never invent a number. Use available data and note explicitly what is missing. Downgrade confidence accordingly.

report_config.description: Controls what appears in the generated report.
